Zepto — AI Feedback Insights — 2026-W28

Why do users repeatedly buy from the same categories?

Users repeatedly buy from the same categories due to convenience, fast delivery, and good prices. However, some users experience issues such as delayed deliveries, poor-quality products, and unhelpful customer support, which can lead to frustration and a switch to rival apps. Despite these issues, many users continue to use Zepto due to its reliability, ease of use, and great deals.

Evidence from reviews:

  • “I’ve had a really great experience using the Zepto app. The delivery is super fast, the interface is clean and easy to use, and the product quality has always been good.”
  • “Zepto is just a wannabe Blinkit at this point. Every single time I order, something goes wrong.”
  • “I have been using Zepto for a while now, and it never ceases to amaze me. The delivery is consistently lightning-fast (usually under 10 minutes!), and the grocery items always arrive fresh.”
  • “Avoid Zepto. This was one of the worst customer support experiences I've had.”

Action ideas:

  • Improve delivery reliability
  • Enhance customer support
  • Increase product quality
  • Simplify navigation and ordering process

Who this helps: Regular customers, New users, Customers with issues, Elderly users

What prevents users from exploring new categories?

Users are prevented from exploring new categories due to issues such as out-of-stock items, inaccurate delivery times, and poor customer support. These problems lead to frustration and mistrust, causing users to switch to other apps. Additionally, the app's limitations, such as limited address options and lack of accessibility features, also hinder users from fully utilizing the app.

Evidence from reviews:

  • “If a product is unavailable, the app should either remove it from the listing or clearly mark it as out of stock instead of displaying it as if it's available.”
  • “Showing pictures of unavailable fruits is misleading and can be upsetting for people who specific”
  • “Zepto was previously much better for visually impaired users, but recent updates have harmed accessibility.”
  • “You are misleading customers by showing various offers. You promote offers like “buy items worth ₹500/₹700 and get limited sale items” (₹1, ₹9, ₹19, ₹29).”

Action ideas:

  • Improve inventory management
  • Enhance customer support
  • Increase accessibility features
  • Provide accurate delivery times
  • Clearly mark out-of-stock items

Who this helps: Visually impaired users, Customers with limited mobility, Users with specific product needs, Customers who value timely delivery, Users who rely on customer support

How do users discover products today?

Users discover products today through various means, including recommendations from others, attractive discounts, and convenient features such as fast delivery and easy-to-use interfaces. However, some users have expressed frustration with misleading offers, poor customer support, and issues with order accuracy and delivery. Despite these criticisms, many users praise Zepto for its quick commerce, affordable prices, and reliable service. The app's ability to offer a wide ran…

Evidence from reviews:

  • “I started to buy milk and other products on someone recommendations.”
  • “I had one of the worst shopping experiences with Zepto.”
  • “Quick commerce is clearly the future, and Zepto is leading the way.”
  • “Very unfair algorithm. They penalize loyal customers.”

Action ideas:

  • Improve customer support
  • Enhance order accuracy
  • Increase transparency in offers and discounts
  • Optimize delivery services

Who this helps: Customers seeking convenient shopping experiences, Users looking for reliable and efficient delivery services, Shoppers seeking affordable prices and discounts, Visually impaired users requiring accessible interfaces

What role do habits play in shopping behavior?

AI analysis of 326 matching reviews (avg 3.0★). Mixed signal (3.0★): 158 critical vs 163 positive reviews. Dominant motifs: “customer support”, “quick delivery”, “quick commerce”, “customer service”. Shopping habits show up as routine reorder and time-of-day journeys (daily / emergency / late-night).

Evidence from reviews:

  • “I have been using Zepto for a while now, and it never ceases to amaze me.”
  • “I am writing this out of sheer frustration.”
  • “I always buy from zepto, I was a regular customer but unfortunately I had to cancel my orders twice so they stopped giving me COD, even after paying online multiple times they are not restoring this f”
  • “zepto is far better than blinkit and any other quick e commerce apps because they not charge any delivery fees, late night fees and even no GST and other charges..”

Action ideas:

  • Design habit-aware journeys for daily staples, emergency, and late-night trips
  • Investigate driver 'customer support' — it clusters tightly in matching feedback
  • Dig into 'delivery' — it appears frequently in matching reviews

Who this helps: Product, UX, Growth

What information do users need before trying a new category?

AI analysis of 1192 matching reviews (avg 2.3★). Critical signal: 804/1192 reviews are ≤2★. Users emphasize friction around “customer support”, “customer service”, “customer care”, “fast delivery”. Before trying something new, users look for quality, price, and trust cues in listings.

Evidence from reviews:

  • “Very good experience.”
  • “Avoid Zepto.”
  • “quality has improved after the first delivery of super stale items.”
  • “I changed my rating and review based on what I am observing since second half of 2025 till today, that is June 2026.”

Action ideas:

  • Surface quality, price, brand, and return cues before first category try
  • Investigate driver 'customer support' — it clusters tightly in matching feedback
  • Dig into 'zepto' — it appears frequently in matching reviews

Who this helps: Product, Merchandising, Trust

What frustrations emerge repeatedly?

AI analysis of 1322 matching reviews (avg 1.4★). Critical signal: 1178/1322 reviews are ≤2★. Users emphasize friction around “customer support”, “customer care”, “customer service”, “delivery partner”. The same operational and support failures recur and crowd out category exploration.

Evidence from reviews:

  • “Zepto is a fraud app, they do delivery wrong items, damaged or spoiled items and their customer care simply denies the return or refund, Main importantly their delivery boy are street rowdies while th”
  • “Very disappointing experience with this grocery app.”
  • “I've seen poorly designed Bots.”
  • “Showing 10 min delivery order it take 15-20 min to delivery order I was order a coconut there is is no water in coconut when I was click on help showing please mail to zepto first after mail customer”

Action ideas:

  • Own the top recurring friction themes across product and ops
  • Investigate driver 'customer support' — it clusters tightly in matching feedback
  • Dig into 'customer' — it appears frequently in matching reviews

Who this helps: Product, Ops, Support

Which user segments are more likely to experiment?

AI analysis of 357 matching reviews (avg 2.2★). Critical signal: 252/357 reviews are ≤2★. Users emphasize friction around “customer support”, “customer service”, “first time”, “customer care”. Experimentation is uneven across cohorts — some try café/pharmacy/offers; others stick to staples. Segment lens: New / first-time users: 99 reviews (avg 1.4★) cite time, order.   • “This is the first time I’m writing a review Zepto is the best instant delivery apps out there easy to deal with no delivery fee or handling charges over 149 great offer and discounts wide variety of p”
  • “What I thought would be a simple Father’s Day order turned into one of the most frustrating customer service experiences I’ve had. I placed an order from Zepto for my father before he had to leave.”
  • “I always buy from zepto, I was a regular customer but unfortunately I had to cancel my orders twice so they stopped giving me COD, even after paying online multiple times they are not restoring this f”

What unmet needs emerge consistently across discussions?

AI analysis of 569 matching reviews (avg 1.6★). Critical signal: 487/569 reviews are ≤2★. Users emphasize friction around “customer support”, “customer service”, “customer care”, “delivery partner”. Recurring unmet needs point to serviceability, reliability, and missing category coverage.

Evidence from reviews:

  • “Best prices and very wide FMCG selection for everyday needs.”
  • “The worst platform I have ever been through.”
  • “Dishonest company and staff.”
  • “I have been using Zepto from last 1 years I liked everything about this app only one thing is annoying me is that the service of Zepto is not spread more areas like blinkit so need to fix this issue f”

Action ideas:

  • Prioritize recurring unmet needs into the product backlog
  • Investigate driver 'customer support' — it clusters tightly in matching feedback
  • Dig into 'order' — it appears frequently in matching reviews

Who this helps: Product, Leadership
